seo.title: Enterprise SaaS Data Extraction AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for data extraction AI agents in enterprise saas. Covers SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for structured data extraction in Enterprise SaaS environments introduces significant compliance obligations across SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, and CCPA frameworks, where a single misconfiguration can trigger SLA breaches or data breach liability. Schema-guided LLM extraction pipelines must enforce strict tenant isolation, field-level accuracy validation, and immutable audit trails to meet enterprise contract requirements. This checklist provides concrete, actionable controls to ensure your extraction agents operate within regulatory boundaries while maintaining the reliability enterprise customers demand.

sections.heading: Data Governance & Tenant Isolation
sections.description: Ensure that extracted data never crosses tenant boundaries and that residency requirements defined in customer contracts are enforced at the infrastructure and application layer.

items.label: Enforce per-tenant data residency routing
items.description: Map each tenant's contract-specified data residency region (e.g., EU, US, APAC) to dedicated extraction pipeline endpoints and storage buckets. Validate routing logic with automated tests before each deployment.

items.label: Implement tenant-scoped LLM context isolation
items.description: Ensure that prompt construction, context windows, and any retrieval-augmented content are strictly scoped to the requesting tenant's data. Use tenant ID as a mandatory partition key in all vector stores and document caches.

items.label: Prohibit cross-tenant prompt caching
items.description: Disable shared prompt or KV caches at the LLM inference layer for enterprise tenants. Validate that no extracted schema fragments, intermediate outputs, or embeddings are reused across tenant boundaries.

items.label: Apply data classification labels to extracted fields
items.description: Tag each extracted field with a sensitivity classification (PII, financial, confidential) based on the source document type and schema definition. Use these labels to enforce downstream access controls and retention policies.

items.label: Validate data residency compliance post-extraction
items.description: After each extraction job, run an automated check confirming that all output artifacts, logs, and intermediate files are stored only in the tenant's contracted region. Alert and halt pipelines on violation.

items.label: Implement tenant-level encryption key management
items.description: Use customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) per tenant for encrypting extracted structured outputs at rest. Ensure key rotation policies align with ISO 27001 Annex A.10 requirements.

items.label: Document data lineage from source document to extracted schema
items.description: Maintain a traceable lineage record linking each extracted field value back to its source document, page, and extraction timestamp. Store lineage metadata in an append-only store for audit purposes.

sections.heading: Schema Validation & Extraction Accuracy
sections.description: Structured output extraction must produce schema-compliant, type-consistent, and complete results. Validation failures must be caught before data reaches downstream systems to prevent silent data corruption.

items.label: Enforce strict JSON schema validation on every LLM output
items.description: Apply a versioned JSON Schema (draft-07 or later) to every extraction response before it is written to any downstream store. Reject and quarantine outputs that fail schema validation rather than silently coercing them.

items.label: Implement field-level type consistency checks
items.description: For each schema field, validate that the extracted value matches the declared type (e.g., ISO 8601 for dates, E.164 for phone numbers, numeric ranges for financial figures). Log type mismatches with the source document reference.

items.label: Define and enforce completeness thresholds per schema
items.description: Set minimum completeness ratios (e.g., 95% of required fields populated) per document type. Flag extractions below threshold for human review rather than passing incomplete records to downstream systems.

items.label: Run confidence scoring on extracted fields
items.description: Instruct the LLM to return a confidence score or uncertainty flag per extracted field. Route low-confidence fields (below a defined threshold) to a human-in-the-loop review queue before finalizing the record.

items.label: Maintain versioned extraction schemas with change control
items.description: Store all extraction schemas in a version-controlled repository with mandatory change review. When a schema changes, re-validate historical extractions against the new schema and document the impact assessment.

items.label: Test extraction accuracy against a labeled ground-truth dataset
items.description: Before deploying a new extraction model or prompt version, evaluate it against a tenant-specific or domain-specific labeled dataset. Require a minimum F1 score threshold (e.g., 0.92) before promotion to production.

items.label: Detect and handle hallucinated field values
items.description: Implement post-extraction checks that cross-reference extracted values against source document text using exact or fuzzy match. Flag fields where the extracted value has no textual basis in the source document.

items.label: Alert on schema drift in incoming documents
items.description: Monitor incoming document structures for deviations from expected formats (e.g., new columns in CSVs, renamed fields in PDFs). Alert operations teams when document structure drift exceeds a defined threshold to prevent silent extraction failures.

sections.heading: Audit Logging & Compliance Traceability
sections.description: Every AI-assisted extraction action must be logged in a tamper-evident, queryable audit trail to satisfy SOC 2 Type II CC7, ISO 27001 A.12.4, and enterprise contract audit rights.

items.label: Log every AI-assisted extraction action with full context
items.description: For each extraction job, write an immutable audit log entry containing: tenant ID, document ID, schema version, model version, prompt hash, extraction timestamp, output hash, and the identity of the initiating user or service account.

items.label: Store audit logs in a write-once, tenant-isolated store
items.description: Direct audit logs to an append-only storage system (e.g., AWS CloudTrail, Azure Immutable Blob Storage) with tenant-level partitioning. Ensure logs cannot be modified or deleted by application-layer processes.

items.label: Capture human review and override actions in the audit trail
items.description: When a human reviewer accepts, rejects, or modifies an AI-extracted field, log the original AI output, the human decision, the reviewer identity, and the timestamp. This is required for SOC 2 CC6.1 and enterprise audit rights.

items.label: Implement audit log retention aligned to contractual and regulatory requirements
items.description: Retain extraction audit logs for a minimum of 12 months online and 7 years in cold storage, or per the customer contract if longer. Automate retention policy enforcement and document it in your SOC 2 control library.

items.label: Expose audit log query API for enterprise customers
items.description: Provide enterprise tenants with a scoped API or UI to query their own audit logs by date range, document ID, or user. This satisfies enterprise contract audit rights and GDPR Article 30 records of processing obligations.

items.label: Alert on anomalous extraction volumes or failure spikes
items.description: Set threshold-based alerts for unusual extraction job volumes, error rate spikes, or repeated schema validation failures per tenant. Route alerts to both the operations team and the tenant's designated contact per SLA terms.

items.label: Include model and prompt version in every audit record
items.description: Record the exact model version (e.g., gpt-4o-2024-08-06) and a hash of the prompt template used for each extraction. This enables reproducibility investigations and supports SOC 2 change management evidence.

sections.heading: SLA Reliability & Operational Safeguards
sections.description: Enterprise SLAs for data extraction typically require 99.9%+ uptime and defined latency SLOs. The AI agent pipeline must include circuit breakers, fallback paths, and capacity controls to prevent SLA breaches.

items.label: Define and instrument SLOs for extraction pipeline latency and throughput
items.description: Set explicit SLOs (e.g., p95 extraction latency < 10s for documents under 20 pages) and instrument them with real-time dashboards. Trigger PagerDuty or equivalent alerts when SLOs are at risk, not after breach.

items.label: Implement circuit breakers for LLM provider outages
items.description: Wrap all LLM API calls in a circuit breaker that trips after a configurable failure threshold. On trip, route jobs to a fallback model or queue them for retry, and notify affected tenants per SLA communication requirements.

items.label: Enforce per-tenant rate limits and quota controls
items.description: Apply per-tenant rate limits on extraction job submissions to prevent a single tenant's burst traffic from degrading service for others. Expose quota usage metrics to tenants via API and dashboard.

items.label: Implement idempotent job submission and deduplication
items.description: Assign a deterministic job ID to each extraction request based on document hash and schema version. Reject or return cached results for duplicate submissions to prevent double-billing and redundant processing.

items.label: Test disaster recovery and data restoration procedures quarterly
items.description: Conduct quarterly DR drills that simulate LLM provider failure, database corruption, and regional outage scenarios. Document RTO and RPO results and include them in SOC 2 evidence packages.

items.label: Validate extraction pipeline integrity after every deployment
items.description: Run a suite of end-to-end extraction tests against known documents with expected outputs after every production deployment. Gate deployments on test pass rate and roll back automatically on failure.

items.label: Communicate planned maintenance and model updates to enterprise tenants
items.description: Provide enterprise tenants with at least 14 days advance notice of planned maintenance windows or LLM model version changes that may affect extraction behavior. Document this process in your SLA addendum.

items.label: Maintain a vendor risk register for LLM and document processing dependencies
items.description: Document all third-party AI and document processing vendors in a risk register with their SOC 2 / ISO 27001 certification status, data processing agreements, and subprocessor notifications. Review quarterly for ISO 27001 A.15 compliance.

notes.note: If extracted data includes personal data of EU residents, document the extraction pipeline as a processing activity in your Article 30 Records of Processing Activities (RoPA). If extraction outputs are used to make automated decisions with legal or significant effects on individuals, implement the right to explanation and human review mechanisms required under Article 22.

For California residents whose personal data is processed during extraction, ensure your pipeline supports data subject access requests (DSARs) that can retrieve or delete specific extracted records by individual. Extraction audit logs must be searchable by data subject identifier to fulfill 45-day DSAR response obligations.

Extraction pipeline access controls must satisfy CC6.1 (logical access restrictions) by enforcing role-based access to extraction jobs, schemas, and outputs. CC7.2 requires that anomalous extraction activity (e.g., bulk data exports, repeated schema failures) is detected and investigated. Ensure your monitoring and alerting controls are included in your SOC 2 control library with evidence of continuous operation.

Annex A.8.2 requires that all data processed by the extraction pipeline is classified and handled according to its sensitivity level. Annex A.12.4 mandates event logging for all information processing systems, which includes LLM API calls, schema validation events, and human review actions. Ensure your ISMS documentation explicitly covers AI-assisted processing activities and that log integrity controls (A.12.4.2) are implemented.

cta.heading: Automate Your Enterprise SaaS Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI extraction pipelines against SOC 2, GDPR, and enterprise SLA requirements—catching schema violations, audit gaps, and tenant isolation failures before they become contract liabilities.
